history.qwen_observation: Tests failed post-migration because Spring Boot was not upgraded, leaving the project on an incompatible version for JDK 21.
history.qwen_rationale: Inserting Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade under JDK 8 and Spring Boot 3.2 + Jakarta migration under JDK 17 aligns the framework with JDK 21 requirements, resolving runtime/test failures caused by deprecated/removed APIs and module restrictions.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at spring_boot_2_7 step because org.junit.rules package is missing, likely due to dependency management changes when upgrading Spring Boot parent/BOM.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly adding junit:junit:4.13.2 with compile scope ensures the JUnit 4 API required by the main source code remains available after the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade, preventing the missing package error and allowing the chain to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at the java8_to_java11 step because the Maven compiler plugin was still configured to use source level 1.7, causing lambda expressions to fail compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing Java8toJava11 with UpgradeToJava11 and explicitly setting maven.compiler.source/target to 11, along with upgrading the compiler plugin to 3.8.1, will ensure the build correctly targets Java 11. Also correcting the JUnit dependency scope to test prevents potential classpath pollution.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at spring_boot_2_7 because JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.rules) are missing from the main source classpath, causing compilation errors in RestServiceRule.java.
history.qwen_rationale: The previous chain added JUnit 4 with test scope, but RestServiceRule resides in src/main/java and requires JUnit 4 at compile time. Changing the dependency scope to compile ensures the JUnit 4 APIs are available during the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade and subsequent Java version bumps.

history.qwen_observation: The java8_to_java11 step failed because the maven-compiler-plugin remained at version 3.5.1 and source/target were still set to 7, causing lambda expressions to fail compilation on JDK 11.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly upgrading the maven-compiler-plugin to 3.11.0 and forcing maven.compiler.source/target properties to 11 will ensure the compiler recognizes Java 11 syntax. This bypasses any parent POM or Spring Boot 2.7 management that might be pinning older versions.

history.qwen_observation: The java8_to_java11 step failed because the Maven compiler plugin was still configured for -source 7, causing lambda expressions to fail compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing UpgradeToJava11 with Java8toJava11 and explicitly setting maven.compiler.source/target to 1.8 ensures the compiler recognizes Java 8 syntax.
